name: Shirley Clay
abstract: Shirley Clay (died February 7, 1951) was an American jazz trumpeter. Clay, who was of African American heritage, gained his early start in St.

about_quotes.speaker: Andrew Blakeney
about_quotes.evidence: I suddenly told Shirley Clay, my second trumpet player, that I was quitting and going to California and I wanted him to take over the band.

about_quotes.speaker: Quentin Jackson
about_quotes.evidence: the trumpets was Langston Curl and Shirley Clay ... And the trombones was Claude Jones and Bennie Morton and myself.

about_quotes.speaker: Preston Jackson
about_quotes.evidence: I had a line of some good fellows like Shirley Clay, George Reynolds, Artis Torks, myself, and George Reynolds.

about_quotes.speaker: Doc Cheatham
about_quotes.evidence: did that in New York with Shirley Clay, and we worked together at the -- at this dancing school down there.

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: Don opened in Connie's with all the musicians that had recorded with him except Red Allen, Leonard Davis and myself. His three trumpets were Sidney de Paris, Shirley Clay and Langston Curl.

about_quotes.speaker: Zilner T. Randolph
about_quotes.evidence: Shirley Clay was going to Chauffeur's Club, so he had me to take the job at the theater... Shirley came to me again and asked me if I wanted a job, if I wanted to come to Chauffeur's.
